title: 03 — Upgrade the Next.js, React, OpenNext, and Cloudflare deployment stack
objective: Move the application to the latest mutually compatible stable versions of Next.js, React, React DOM, the OpenNext Cloudflare adapter, Wrangler, and directly related packages.
userContext: Maintainers need the application and deployment stack to stay current while preserving routing, rendering, deployment metadata, Cloudflare Worker builds, and existing repository architecture.
currentBehaviour: The framework and deployment packages may lag current stable compatible releases and may require coordinated migration because Next.js, React, OpenNext, Wrangler, generated types, and deployment workflows interact closely.
requiredBehaviour: The repository must run on a current stable mutually compatible Next.js, React, OpenNext, Wrangler, and Cloudflare deployment set, with official migrations applied where needed and all existing application and deployment behaviours preserved.
userExperience: Users continue to search, view, copy, and create artifact variations without visible regression. Maintainers can build, preview, and deploy the Cloudflare Worker using the upgraded framework stack and documented commands.
functionalRequirements: Determine the latest stable mutually compatible release set at implementation time.
Upgrade eslint-config-next where framework compatibility requires it.
Upgrade React type packages where they must align with the selected React versions.
Upgrade directly related Cloudflare or framework packages required for compatibility.
Review official migration documentation and release notes for every major-version change.
Use official codemods where appropriate, while reviewing all generated modifications.
Remove deprecated framework configuration and APIs.
Preserve existing application behaviour and repository architecture unless a migration requires an explicit design change.
Regenerate relevant generated types or configuration using repository commands.
Update repository documentation where commands, prerequisites, configuration, or behaviour changed.
technicalConsiderations: This feature depends on Feature 01 for Node.js 24 and npm 11 and should follow Feature 02 so workflow action compatibility is already modernised.
Do not combine unrelated ESLint-major migration work with this feature unless strictly required by the chosen Next.js release.
Verify local development startup, linting, type checking, tests, Next.js production build, OpenNext Worker build, Cloudflare preview, and Cloudflare deployment.

Verify application routing and rendering, deployment identity generation and display, commit and pull-request links, browser-local rendering of deployment timestamps, and explicit development-build state.
Generated Cloudflare types or framework configuration must be produced by repository commands rather than manually edited where generation is expected.
If a selected stable release is incompatible with another required package, record the compatibility constraint and the latest tested mutually compatible version.
outOfScope: Unrelated ESLint-major migration work unless strictly required by the selected Next.js release.
Broad application redesign or repository architecture changes not required by migration.
Refreshing unrelated direct dependencies reserved for Feature 05.
Changing the Node.js or npm baseline established by Feature 01.
acceptanceCriteria: The selected Next.js, React, React DOM, OpenNext Cloudflare adapter, Wrangler, and directly related package versions are documented as mutually compatible stable releases.
Official migration documentation and release notes for major-version changes have been reviewed and relevant changes applied.
Deprecated framework configuration and APIs within scope are removed or replaced.
Application routing, rendering, artifact browsing, copying, variation flow, and theme behaviour remain functional.
Deployment identity generation and display continue to work, including commit links, pull-request links, browser-local timestamps, and development-build fallback.
Linting, type checking, tests, Next.js production build, OpenNext Worker build, Cloudflare preview, and deployment validation pass or have documented external-service limitations.
Generated types or configuration are regenerated with repository commands.
Documentation reflects changed commands, prerequisites, configuration, or behaviour.
No unrelated dependency refresh or broad refactoring is included.
codexGuidance: Implement after DEV-002. Treat the framework and deployment packages as one compatibility set, use official migration sources, and keep unrelated linting and dependency maintenance for DEV-004 and DEV-005 unless required for this migration.
